meta_description: Since its invention in the 1980s, 3D printing has become increasingly prevalent in the manufacturing industry.

sections.heading: The Top Challenges in Additive Manufacturing and How to Overcome Them
sections.text: The Top Challenges in Additive Manufacturing and How to Overcome Them Published on October 26, 2022 Originally published on fastradius.com on October 26, 2022 3D printing , also known as additive manufacturing, is a relatively new technology, but it has already come a long way. Since its invention in the 1980s, 3D printing has become increasingly prevalent in the manufacturing industry, so much so that, in 2021, the global 3D printing market was valued at $12.6 billion , and expected to grow to $34.8 billion by 2026. Although companies like Adidas and Rawlings have used 3D printing technology to create innovative new products, additive manufacturing continues to face adoption challenges at...
